vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Carries wing spar loads into fuselage spine
Dissipates heat from avionics bay to fuselage structure
Routes electrical signals from avionics bay to wiring harness

risk.desc: Thermal management of avionics bay may be insufficient for high-altitude operations.
risk.mit: Consider adding additional cooling channels or heat sinks within the avionics bay.

risk.desc: Structural reinforcement ribs may not be sufficient for high-g maneuvers.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness or number of structural reinforcement ribs.

risk.desc: Service access panel may be difficult to reach for maintenance in high-altitude operations.
risk.mit: Design the service access panel to be more accessible or include remote maintenance features.

risk.desc: Landing gear mount may experience excessive wear due to repeated use.
risk.mit: Use higher quality materials or increase the durability of the landing gear mount.

risk.desc: Belly camera window may be prone to damage during landing.
risk.mit: Increase the protective measures around the belly camera window.

requirements: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin
Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high-altitude operation.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal analysis and apply appropriate thermal management solutions.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for moving parts.
risk.mit: Review and ensure all moving parts have adequate clearance.

risk.desc: Possibility of misalignment during assembly due to tolerance stack-up.
risk.mit: Implement precise assembly procedures and conduct alignment checks.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to unexpected loads.
risk.mit: Perform load analysis and design for safety factors.

risk.desc: Potential for inadequate service access for maintenance.
risk.mit: Ensure service access panels are strategically placed and easily accessible.

risk.desc: Thermal management for the ESC module is not explicitly detailed.
risk.mit: Add a heat sink to the ESC module to improve thermal management.

risk.desc: The harness routing path may cause drag during flight.
risk.mit: Optimize the harness routing path to reduce drag.

risk.desc: The bolt spacing and alignment for the nacelle mounting flange may not be optimized.

risk.desc: The rotor seat may not have sufficient clearance for the rotor.
risk.mit: Ensure the rotor seat has sufficient clearance for the rotor.

risk.desc: The wing spar socket may not be optimized for the nacelle mounting flange.
risk.mit: Optimize the wing spar socket for the nacelle mounting flange.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: tilting rotor nacelle with 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.path_description: Rotor thrust load 20-200N is transferred from the rotor to the nacelle via the tilt axis and bearing seats into the wing structure
Servo-driven worm gear rotates the nacelle from 0-90deg tilt axis
Motor and ESC modules can be accessed and replaced through access covers

integration_opportunities.opportunity_description: Nacelle mounting flange can be optimized for bolt spacing and alignment with the wing spar socket
Servo harness can be routed through the nacelle for cleaner installation and reduced drag
Thermal management can be improved by adding a heat sink to the ESC module

generated_parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
generated_parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

verify.rule: schema must be complete
mass must be within budget
P0 internal_feature_targets must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops
interface fit_class and stack_up_mm must be defined
locking/preload evidence must be visible
boundary_conditions must be defined
coordinates must be distributed across the subsystem volume
must use drill, pocket, boss, channel, cylinder, chamfer, fillet, pattern, and engrave
motion claims must be grounded in a named mechanism type
tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance must be visible
wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths must be separate and inspectable
output must remain an educational mockup
tilt mechanism loads must route through bearing seats into wing structure
a primary datum feature must be explicitly named and placed
interfaces must include fit_class and stack_up_mm
cad_brief must include ordered assembly_sequence
must use seed-characteristic ops
dimensions must match tiltrotor scale

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion issues due to thin-air optimized props
risk.mit: Implement thermal expansion compensation in design

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling for high-altitude operation
risk.mit: Increase cooling fin surface area

risk.desc: Risk of material fatigue in thin-air conditions
risk.mit: Use higher-grade materials for critical components

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access for high-altitude maintenance
risk.mit: Ensure access covers are easily removable

risk.desc: Risk of rotor imbalance due to manufacturing tolerances
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilt axis with bearing seats, servo linkage, rotation clearance arc

physics_paths.path: Rotor thrust -> Tilt axis -> Bearing seats -> Wing structure
Servo rotation -> Servo linkage -> Tilt axis rotation
Servo heat -> Tilt servo housing -> Air cooling
Servo replacement -> Access cover -> Servo mounting
Maintenance access -> Access cover -> Servo mounting

Arc for tilt axis rotation clearance

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate servo linkage with servo mount
Integrate cooling fins with servo mount

verification_focus.description: Verify tilt axis rotation range and clearance
Verify servo mount stability under load

risk.desc: Potential clearance issues with snap-fit interfaces due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter tolerance control and conduct fit tests during prototype phase.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient preload on bolted interfaces leading to loosening over time.
risk.mit: Conduct regular maintenance checks and implement torque verification procedures.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion may cause stress on the V-tail structure during high-altitude operations.
risk.mit: Include thermal expansion joints and conduct thermal analysis during design phase.

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment during assembly due to complex interface geometry.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and conduct training for assembly personnel.

risk.desc: Risk of damage to the ruddervator mechanism during field maintenance due to limited access.
risk.mit: Design additional access panels and conduct regular maintenance checks.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure

physics_paths.path: Loads from nacelle tilt mechanism are routed through the V-tail ruddervator into the wing spar and fuselage spine
Landing gear loads are transferred through the landing gear mount into the V-tail ruddervator structure
Thermal loads from sun exposure are dissipated through the V-tail ruddervator into the fuselage spine
Motion from the MG996R servo is transmitted through the hinge piano wire into the ruddervator for control
Human access for maintenance and inspection of the ruddervator and servo is provided through an access panel

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Integration of wing spar into V-tail structure

Integration of ruddervator mechanism into V-tail structure
Integration of stabilizer into V-tail structure
Integration of servo mount into V-tail structure
Integration of access panel into V-tail structure
Integration of hinge piano wire into V-tail structure
Integration of servo linkage into V-tail structure

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: H7/h6 fit class for bolted interfaces, LC2 for snap-fit interfaces
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for FDM, orientation for CNC
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Coordinate-referenced CMM inspection, visual inspection for surface finish
